mapped.auth: API key comes from `--api-key` CLI flag OR `ACESTEP_API_KEY` env. server_cli.py:36-39 defines the flag (default = env), and server_cli.py:70-71 writes the flag value back into `os.environ["ACESTEP_API_KEY"]` BEFORE uvicorn starts; create_app() reads `ACESTEP_API_KEY` and calls set_api_key() (api_server.py:190-192). If unset, ALL auth checks are disabled (acestep/api/http/auth.py:39-40, 67-68). Two enforcement paths: (1) `verify_api_key` FastAPI dependency for header-only endpoints (auth.py:57-75): requires `Authorization` header, accepts either the raw key or `Bearer <key>` (prefix stripped at auth.py:73); 401 `{"detail":"Missing Authorization header"}` or `{"detail":"Invalid API key"}`. (2) `verify_token_from_request` for legacy body-parsing endpoints (/release_task, /query_result, /create_random_sample, /format_input, /v1/create_sample) (auth.py:23-54): checks body field `ai_token` first, else `Authorization` header (Bearer or raw); 401 `Invalid ai_token` / `Invalid API key` / `Missing ai_token or Authorization header`. IMPORTANT EXCEPTION: the OpenRouter routes `POST /v1/chat/completions` and `GET /v1/models` have NO auth dependency at all (openrouter_adapter.py:594, 653). CORS: only localhost origins allowed — allow_origins ["null","http://localhost","http://127.0.0.1"] plus regex ^https?://(localhost|127.0.0.1)(:port)?$, methods GET/POST/OPTIONS, headers Content-Type/Authorization (route_setup.py:62-68). No request-body size limit middleware exists anywhere (only CORSMiddleware is added, route_setup.py:62); uvicorn/Starlette impose no default body cap, multipart uploads are streamed to temp files in 1MB chunks (release_task_audio_paths.py:76-81).

endpoints.purpose: Create an async music-generation job (the primary generation endpoint). Also handles analysis-only modes (LM metadata plan, full audio analysis, code extraction) via flags.
endpoints.request: Accepts application/json, multipart/form-data, application/x-www-form-urlencoded, or raw-body JSON/querystring sniffing; otherwise 415 (release_task_request_parser.py:88-205). Every field has aliases (PARAM_ALIASES, release_task_param_parser.py:9-46) and may also be supplied inside nested `param_obj` (JSON string or object) or `metas`/`meta`/`metadata`/`user_metadata` (release_task_param_parser.py:104-141). Canonical fields (type / default / required / validation): ai_token: str, optional auth (auth.py:42-46). prompt (alias caption): str "" — caption text. global_caption: str "". lyrics: str "" ("[inst]"/"[instrumental]"/empty => instrumental, server_utils.py:135-143). thinking: bool false — LM generates audio codes (lm-dit). sample_mode: bool false — LM auto-generates caption/lyrics/metas. sample_query (aliases sampleQuery, description, desc): str "" — triggers create_sample when non-empty. use_format (alias format): bool false — LM-enhance caption/lyrics. model (aliases model_name, dit_model): str|null — DiT model selection. bpm: int|null. key_scale (aliases keyscale, key): str "". time_signature (alias timesignature): str "". vocal_language (alias language): str "en". inference_steps: int 8. guidance_scale: float 7.0. use_random_seed: bool true. seed: int|str -1 — comma-separated string gives per-take seeds (job_generation_setup.py:80-96); used only when use_random_seed=false. reference_audio_path (alias ref_audio_path): str|null — absolute paths only allowed inside system temp dir, traversal blocked, else 400 (release_task_audio_paths.py:14-46). src_audio_path (alias ctx_audio_path): str|null — same validation. In multipart, file fields `ref_audio`/`reference_audio` and `ctx_audio`/`src_audio` are saved to temp files and used instead (release_task_request_parser.py:110-126). audio_duration (aliases duration, target_duration): float|null — null/<=0 => auto (-1 sentinel, job_generation_setup.py:168). batch_size: int|null — NUMBER OF TAKES; defaults to 2 when omitted (job_generation_setup.py:203). repainting_start: float 0.0. repainting_end: float|null (null=>-1). instruction: str = "Fill the audio semantic mask based on the given conditions:" (constants.py:119); auto-resolved per task_type/track_name/track_classes (job_generation_setup.py:25-67). audio_cover_strength (alias cover_strength): float 1.0. cover_noise_strength: float 0.0 (0=pure noise, 1=closest to source). audio_code_string (alias audio_codes): str "" — pre-computed semantic codes, skips LM code generation. task_type: str "text2music" — one of text2music|repaint|cover|cover-nofsq|extract|lego|complete (constants.py:82,89; turbo models support only the first 4). chunk_mask_mode: Literal explicit|auto, default auto (pydantic-validated, release_task_models.py:65). repaint_latent_crossfade_frames: int 10. repaint_wav_crossfade_sec: float 0.0. repaint_mode: Literal conservative|balanced|aggressive, default balanced. repaint_strength: float 0.5, ge=0.0 le=1.0 (release_task_models.py:78-83). analysis_only: bool false — LM metadata plan only, no audio. full_analysis_only: bool false — encode src audio + LM understanding. extract_codes_only: bool false — requires src_audio_path; returns codes only. use_adg: bool false. cfg_interval_start: float 0.0. cfg_interval_end: float 1.0. infer_method: str "ode" ("ode"|"sde", not enforced). shift: float 3.0 (doc range 1.0-5.0, base models only; not enforced). timesteps: str|null — comma-separated floats; overrides inference_steps+shift (server_utils.py:124-132). audio_format: str "mp3" — flac|mp3|opus|aac|wav|wav32; unsupported values silently fall back to flac (audio_utils.py:129-132). use_tiled_decode: bool true. lm_model_path: str|null. lm_backend: Literal vllm|pt|mlx default vllm (release_task_models.py:109). constrained_decoding: bool true. constrained_decoding_debug: bool false. use_cot_caption (alias cot_caption): bool true. use_cot_language (alias cot_language): bool true. is_format_caption: bool false. allow_lm_batch (alias parallel_thinking): bool true. track_name: str|null. track_classes (alias instruments): list[str]|null (bare string coerced to 1-elem list, release_task_request_builder.py:35-37). lm_temperature: float 0.85. lm_cfg_scale: float 2.5. lm_top_k: int|null (<=0 treated as off). lm_top_p: float 0.9 (>=1.0 treated as off). lm_repetition_penalty: float 1.0. lm_negative_prompt: str "NO USER INPUT".
endpoints.response: Standard envelope {data, code:200, error:null, timestamp:<ms epoch int>, extra:null} (api_server.py:120-128) with data = {task_id: str(uuid4), status: "queued", queue_position: int (1-based pending count)} (release_task_route.py:85). Errors: 429 {detail:"Server busy: queue is full"} when asyncio.Queue full (maxsize env ACESTEP_QUEUE_MAXSIZE default 200; release_task_route.py:68-74, api_server.py:194); 400 malformed JSON / non-object payload / bad audio path; 401 auth; 415 unsupported content type.
endpoints.notes: Default batch_size=2 means TWO takes per request unless you pass batch_size=1 explicitly. Wired into api_server via configure_api_routes (route_setup.py:134-148).

endpoints.purpose: Batch-poll task status/results for one or more task IDs.
endpoints.request: JSON or form body: {task_id_list: JSON-array-string or actual list of task_id strings (default "[]"; non-parseable => []), ai_token?: str} (query_result_route.py:47-55, query_result_service.py:10-26).
endpoints.response: Envelope with data = ordered list, one item per task_id: {task_id: str, result: str (JSON-ENCODED array — must be JSON.parse'd by client), status: int, progress_text?: str (last captured server log line)}. status int: 0=queued/running, 1=succeeded, 2=failed/unknown (server_utils.py:10,118-121). Unknown task_id => {task_id, result:"[]", status:0} (query_result_service.py:195). Parsed `result` array items — RUNNING (from local cache): [{file:"", wave:"", status:0, create_time:int(s), env:"development", progress: float 0..1, stage: str}] (local_cache_updates.py:148-156); store-derived running/failed adds prompt:"", lyrics:"", metas:{}, progress, stage, error: str|null (full Python traceback on failure) (query_result_service.py:119-131). SUCCESS: one element PER GENERATED TAKE: {file: "/v1/audio?path=<url-encoded absolute path>" (already a download URL), wave:"", status:1, create_time:int, env, prompt: str (final caption), lyrics: str (final lyrics), metas:{bpm, duration, genres, keyscale, timesignature} (cache variant also has metas.prompt/metas.lyrics + top-level generation_info: str(markdown timing summary), seed_value:"s1,s2", lm_model, dit_model, progress:1.0, stage:"succeeded") } (query_result_service.py:84-117; local_cache_updates.py:79-114). analysis/extract modes: result_data=[full result dict] with status_message "Full Hardware Analysis Success" or "Audio Codes Extraction Success", audio_codes, metas, bpm/keyscale/duration/etc (query_result_service.py:76-80; job_analysis_runtime.py:45-114). Cache-staleness rule: running item older than 3600s reported as status 2 (query_result_service.py:53-54, api_server.py:111).

endpoints.purpose: Download generated audio bytes by file path (the only audio-serving endpoint).
endpoints.request: Query param `path`: URL-encoded absolute file path (exactly what /query_result returns in the `file` field / audio_paths). Auth: Authorization header (Depends verify_api_key).
endpoints.response: FileResponse bytes. media_type by extension: .mp3 audio/mpeg, .wav audio/wav, .flac audio/flac, .ogg audio/ogg, default audio/mpeg. 403 if resolved path is outside app.state.temp_audio_dir; 404 if not a file; 401 auth.

endpoints.purpose: Liveness/readiness probe. NO auth.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {status:"ok" (always, even when models not loaded), service:"ACE-Step API", version:"1.0", models_initialized: bool, llm_initialized: bool, loaded_model: str|null (primary DiT name e.g. acestep-v15-turbo), loaded_lm_model: str|null}.

endpoints.purpose: Queue/job statistics.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {jobs:{total:int,queued:int,running:int,succeeded:int,failed:int}, queue_size:int, queue_maxsize:int (default 200), avg_job_seconds:float (rolling mean of last 50 jobs, initial 5.0)}.

endpoints.purpose: SHADOWED ROUTE — two handlers registered; the OpenRouter one wins because its router is included first (route_setup.py:70-71 before register_model_service_routes at 73). Actual behavior: OpenRouter-format model list, NO auth.
endpoints.response: {object:"list", data:[{id:"acestep/<model>", name, created, input_modalities:["text","audio"], output_modalities:["audio","text"], context_length:4096, max_output_length:300, pricing:{prompt,completion,request,image:"0"}, description, architecture:null}]} — only models whose handler slot is initialized appear (empty list before init). NOT wrapped in the {data,code,...} envelope.
endpoints.evidence: acestep/openrouter_adapter.py:594-651; acestep/openrouter_models.py:205-236; shadowed handler acestep/api/http/model_service_routes.py:179-183; ordering acestep/api/route_setup.py:70-84
endpoints.notes: For the wrapped inventory payload use /v1/model_inventory instead.

endpoints.purpose: Internal (non-OpenRouter) DiT/LM model inventory: what exists in checkpoints/ and what is loaded.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {models:[{name:str, is_default:bool, is_loaded:bool, supported_task_types:[str] (turbo: text2music,repaint,cover,cover-nofsq; base adds extract,lego,complete — read from each model's config.json is_turbo)}], default_model:str|null, lm_models:[{name:str,is_loaded:bool}] (dirs starting acestep-5Hz-lm-), loaded_lm_model:str|null, llm_initialized:bool}.

endpoints.purpose: On-demand initialize/switch DiT model (slots 1-3) and optionally the 5Hz LM. Runs in thread executor (does not block event loop).
endpoints.request: JSON InitModelRequest: {model?: str|null (DiT name e.g. 'acestep-v15-base'), slot?: int|null ge=1 le=3 (pydantic-validated; slots 2/3 require ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH2/3 set at startup), init_llm?: bool=false, lm_model_path?: str|null (e.g. 'acestep-5Hz-lm-1.7B')}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message:"Model initialization completed", slot:int, loaded_model:str|null, loaded_lm_model:str|null, models:[...], lm_models:[...], llm_initialized:bool}. Failures returned with HTTP 200 but envelope code=400 (slot unavailable) or 500 + error string.

endpoints.purpose: Return a random canned example payload (no LM call) for pre-filling UI forms.
endpoints.request: JSON or form: {sample_type?: str = "simple_mode" (anything else => custom mode), ai_token?}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = one example JSON loaded from examples/simple_mode/example_*.json — shape {description:str, instrumental:bool, vocal_language:str} — or examples/text2music/example_*.json — shape {think:bool, caption:str, lyrics:str, bpm:int, duration:int, keyscale:str, language:str, timesignature:str}. code=500 + error "No example data available" if none loaded.

endpoints.purpose: LM query-rewrite step exposed standalone: enhance/normalize caption+lyrics and fill metadata via the 5Hz LM (format_sample). Lazy-initializes the LM if needed.
endpoints.request: JSON or form: {prompt?: str "", lyrics?: str "", temperature?: float 0.85, param_obj?: dict-or-JSON-string {duration?: float, bpm?: int, key?/key_scale?: str, time_signature?: str, language?: str}, time_signature?: str (top-level fallback), ai_token?}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {caption: str, lyrics: str, bpm: int|null, key_scale: str, time_signature: str, duration: float|null, vocal_language: str ("unknown" fallback)}. Errors: 503 detail "LLM not initialized. Set ACESTEP_INIT_LLM=true in .env to enable." when lazy-load disabled; 500 "LLM init failed: ..."; envelope code=500 + error on format failure.

endpoints.purpose: LM planner exposed standalone: natural-language description -> full generation plan (caption, lyrics, bpm, key, duration, time signature, language) via 5Hz LM create_sample. API twin of Gradio Simple-Mode 'Create Sample'.
endpoints.request: JSON or form: {query?: str "", instrumental?: bool false (string 'true'/'1'/'yes' accepted), vocal_language?: str "unknown", temperature?: float 0.85, ai_token?}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {caption: str, lyrics: str, bpm: int|null, keyscale: str, duration: float|null, timesignature: str, vocal_language: str}. Same 503/500 LM-init errors as /format_input; envelope code=500 on create failure.

endpoints.purpose: OpenRouter/OpenAI-compatible music generation (same job queue). Returns audio as base64 data URL inline. NO auth. Does NOT lazy-init models (503 if not initialized).
endpoints.request: JSON ChatCompletionRequest (extra fields allowed): model: str REQUIRED ("acestep/<name>"); messages: [{role: system|user|assistant, content: str | [{type:text,text}|{type:input_audio,input_audio:{data: base64, format:"mp3"}}], name?}] REQUIRED — only last user message parsed; <prompt>/<lyrics> tags or lyric-heuristics split text; audio blobs become temp files routed by task_type (cover/repaint/lego/extract/complete: audio[0]->src, audio[1]->reference; else audio[0]->reference). modalities?: [str]; audio_config?: {duration?: float, format: str "mp3", bpm?: int, key_scale?: str, time_signature?: str, vocal_language?: str, instrumental?: bool}; temperature?: float ge0 le2; top_p?: float ge0 le1; top_k?: int ge0; max_tokens?: int ge1 (unused); stream: bool false; stop?; seed?: int|str (comma-separated for batch; null => random); thinking?: bool; guidance_scale?: float; batch_size?: int (null => 1 here, NOT 2); lyrics: str "" (direct, bypasses parsing); sample_mode: bool false; use_format: bool false; use_cot_caption: bool true; use_cot_language: bool true; task_type: str "text2music"; repainting_start: float 0.0; repainting_end?: float; audio_cover_strength: float 1.0; audio_codes: str ""; cover_noise_strength: float 0.0; inference_steps: int 8; infer_method: str "ode"; lm_cfg_scale: float 2.0; use_cot_metas?: bool.
endpoints.response: Non-streaming: {id:"chatcmpl-...", object:"chat.completion", created, model, choices:[{index:0, message:{role:"assistant", content: markdown w/ '## Metadata'+'## Lyrics', audio:[{type:"audio_url",audio_url:{url:"data:audio/...;base64,..."}}]|null (first take only), audio_codes: str|null}, finish_reason:"stop"}], usage:{0,0,0}}. Streaming (stream:true): SSE chat.completion.chunk events; heartbeat '.' content chunk every 2s; then content chunk, audio chunk, optional '[audio_codes]...[/audio_codes]' chunk, finish_reason stop, 'data: [DONE]'. Errors: 400 invalid request / no prompt-lyrics-query-audio; 429 queue full; 503 model not initialized; 504 after ACESTEP_GENERATION_TIMEOUT (default 600s, non-streaming only); 500 with rec.error detail on failure.

endpoints.purpose: Load LoRA/LoKr adapter into primary model.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, lora_path, adapter_name?}. 500 if model not initialized; 400 detail on backend non-success; 500 on exception.

endpoints.purpose: Unload adapter, restore base model.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message}. 400/500 as above.

endpoints.purpose: Enable/disable loaded LoRA.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, use_lora}; failures via envelope code=400/500.

endpoints.purpose: Set adapter strength.
endpoints.request: JSON {scale: float REQUIRED ge=0.0 le=1.0 (pydantic), adapter_name?: str|null}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, scale, adapter_name?}; failures envelope code=400/500.

endpoints.purpose: Current adapter state.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {lora_loaded: bool, use_lora: bool, lora_scale: float, adapter_type: str|null, scales: {}, active_adapter, adapters: [], synthetic_default_mode: bool}. 500 if model not initialized.

endpoints.purpose: Reload components unloaded during training/preprocessing (DiT/VAE/text-encoder, LLM, decoder back to GPU).
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message: str, reloaded: [str]}. 500 if service not initialized; envelope code=500 on failure.

endpoints.purpose: Start LoRA training from preprocessed tensors (background thread).
endpoints.request: JSON StartTrainingRequest: tensor_dir: str REQUIRED; lora_rank: int 64 (1-256); lora_alpha: int 128 (1-512); lora_dropout: float 0.1 (0-1); learning_rate: float 1e-4 (>0); train_epochs: int 10 (>=1); train_batch_size: int 1 (>=1); gradient_accumulation: int 4 (>=1); save_every_n_epochs: int 5 (>=1); training_shift: float 3.0 (>=0); training_seed: int 42; lora_output_dir: str "./lora_output"; use_fp8: bool false; gradient_checkpointing: bool false.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message:"Training started", tensor_dir, output_dir, config:{lora_rank,lora_alpha,learning_rate,epochs}, fp8_enabled}. 400 if already training; 500 model/decoder not initialized; envelope code=500 on trainer error.

endpoints.purpose: Start LoKr training.
endpoints.request: JSON StartLoKRTrainingRequest: tensor_dir: str REQUIRED; lokr_linear_dim: int 64 (1-256); lokr_linear_alpha: int 128 (1-512); lokr_factor: int -1; lokr_decompose_both/use_tucker/use_scalar: bool false; lokr_weight_decompose: bool true; learning_rate: float 0.03 (>0); train_epochs: int 500 (>=1); train_batch_size: int 1; gradient_accumulation: int 4; save_every_n_epochs: int 5; training_shift: float 3.0; training_seed: int 42; output_dir: str "./lokr_output"; gradient_checkpointing: bool false.
endpoints.response: Analogous wrapped start message.

endpoints.purpose: Request training stop and reset training state.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message:"Stopping training..."|"No training in progress"}.

endpoints.purpose: Training progress snapshot.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {is_training: bool, should_stop: bool, current_step: int, current_loss: float|null, status: str, config: {}, tensor_dir: str, loss_history: [{step,loss}] (last 1000), tensorboard_url: str|null, tensorboard_logdir: str|null, training_log: str, start_time: float|null, current_epoch: int, steps_per_second: float, estimated_time_remaining: float, error: str|null}.

endpoints.purpose: Inspect a preprocessed tensor directory (manifest.json or .pt scan).
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {dataset_name, num_samples, custom_tag, tensor_dir, message}. 400 if missing/not dir/no .pt files.

endpoints.purpose: Copy trained LoRA/LoKr weights ('final' or latest epoch checkpoint) to a destination directory.
endpoints.request: JSON ExportLoRARequest {export_path: str REQUIRED, lora_output_dir: str REQUIRED}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, export_path, source}. 404 if no checkpoints; envelope code=500 on copy failure.

endpoints.purpose: Scan directory of audio files into a new in-memory training dataset.
endpoints.request: JSON ScanDirectoryRequest {audio_dir: str REQUIRED, dataset_name: str "my_lora_dataset", custom_tag: str "", tag_position: str "replace" (prepend/append/replace), all_instrumental: bool true}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, num_samples, samples:[{index,filename,audio_path,duration,caption,genre,prompt_override,lyrics,bpm,keyscale,timesignature,language,is_instrumental,labeled}]}. code=400 if none found.

endpoints.purpose: Load existing dataset JSON.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, dataset_name, num_samples, labeled_count, samples:[...]}; code=400/500 variants keep same data shape.

endpoints.purpose: Synchronously auto-label all samples via DiT+LM (blocks until done).
endpoints.request: JSON AutoLabelRequest {skip_metas: bool false, format_lyrics: bool false, transcribe_lyrics: bool false, only_unlabeled: bool false, lm_model_path?: str, save_path?: str, chunk_size: int 16 (>=1, legacy aliases hunk_size/hunksize), batch_size: int 1 (>=1, alias batchsize)}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, labeled_count, samples:[...]}. 400 no dataset; 500 model/LLM not initialized.

endpoints.purpose: Start auto-label as a background thread task.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {task_id: str(uuid4), message, total: int}. Poll via auto_label_status.

endpoints.purpose: Poll one auto-label task.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {task_id, status: running|completed|failed, progress: str, current: int, total: int, save_path, last_updated_index, last_updated_sample, result?: {...} (completed), error?: str (failed)}. 404 unknown task.

endpoints.purpose: Poll latest auto-label task (or idle).
endpoints.response: Same shape; status "idle" + empty fields when none.

endpoints.purpose: Synchronously preprocess loaded dataset to training tensors.
endpoints.request: JSON PreprocessDatasetRequest {output_dir: str REQUIRED, skip_existing: bool false}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, output_dir, num_tensors}. 400 no dataset; 500 model not initialized; code=400/500 on failure.

endpoints.purpose: Start preprocessing as background thread task.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {task_id, message, total}.

endpoints.purpose: Poll one preprocess task.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {task_id, status: running|completed|failed, progress: str, current: int, total: int, result?: {message,output_dir,num_tensors}, error?: str}. 404 unknown.

endpoints.purpose: Poll latest preprocess task (or idle).
endpoints.response: Same shape; status "idle" when none.

endpoints.purpose: Persist current dataset to JSON.
endpoints.request: JSON SaveDatasetRequest {save_path: str REQUIRED, dataset_name: str "my_lora_dataset", custom_tag?: str, tag_position?: str, all_instrumental?: bool, genre_ratio?: int 0-100}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, save_path}. 400 no dataset / backend error.

endpoints.purpose: List all samples in current dataset.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {dataset_name, num_samples, labeled_count, samples:[...]}. 400 no dataset.

endpoints.purpose: Get one sample by index.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = sample.to_dict() + {index}. 404 out of range; 400 no dataset.

endpoints.purpose: Update one sample's labels.
endpoints.request: Path param sample_idx + JSON UpdateSampleRequest {sample_idx: int REQUIRED ge=0 (duplicated in body), caption: str "", genre: str "", prompt_override?: str|null, lyrics: str "[Instrumental]", bpm?: int, keyscale: str "", timesignature: str "", language: str "unknown", is_instrumental: bool true}.
endpoints.response: Envelope data = {message, sample: {...}+index}. 400/500 variants.

mapped.task_lifecycle: CREATE: POST /release_task parses the body, validates auth, then store.create() makes an in-memory _JobRecord {job_id: str(uuid4), status:\"queued\", created_at, progress:0.0, stage:\"queued\", progress_text:\"\", env:\"development\", result:null, error:null} (jobs/store.py:18-91) and the (job_id, GenerateMusicRequest) tuple is put on a process-local asyncio.Queue (maxsize env ACESTEP_QUEUE_MAXSIZE, default 200 — 429 when full). Response data: {task_id, status:\"queued\", queue_position} (release_task_route.py:66-85). The store is in-memory only — uvicorn is hard-pinned to workers=1 (server_cli.py:89-96). EXECUTE: ACESTEP_QUEUE_WORKERS (default 1) worker coroutines consume the queue (worker_runtime.py:42-73, jobs/worker_loops.py:9-57). For each job: (1) ensure_models_initialized — with default ACESTEP_NO_INIT=true this lazily downloads+loads DiT/VAE/LM on the FIRST job, synchronously on the event loop (job_runtime_state.py:11-61, startup_model_init.py:185); the job remains status \"queued\" during this. (2) store.mark_running (progress clamped >=0.01, stage \"running\") + a 'running' record written to the local file cache under key \"ace_step_v1.5_<task_id>\" with 7-day TTL (api_server.py:109-110). (3) Blocking generation runs in a ThreadPoolExecutor (job_execution_runtime.py:74-77): LM prep (sample_mode/use_format/thinking), optional analysis-only short-circuits, then generate_music; a throttled progress callback (fires on >=0.01 delta, stage change, or 0.5s elapsed) calls store.update_progress(job_id, value 0.0-1.0, stage) and rewrites the cache progress record (job_blocking_generation.py:132-159). COMPLETE: on success store.mark_succeeded(result) sets progress=1.0, stage=\"succeeded\", finished_at, and the terminal result is written to the cache (one entry per audio take). The success result dict is {first_audio_path: \"/v1/audio?path=...\"|null, second_audio_path, audio_paths: [download URLs], raw_audio_paths: [absolute disk paths], generation_info: markdown timing string, status_message, seed_value: \"s1,s2,...\", prompt (final caption), lyrics (final), metas: {bpm,duration,genres,keyscale,timesignature,prompt(original),lyrics(original)} (\"N/A\" scrubbing for missing/negative), bpm: int|null, duration: number|null, genres/keyscale/timesignature: str|null, lm_model, dit_model, cot_caption, cot_lyrics} (job_result_payload.py:54-137). On failure store.mark_failed(error=FULL TRACEBACK STRING) stage=\"failed\" (job_execution_runtime.py:89-103). POLL: POST /query_result (see endpoint entry) — per-task item {task_id, result: JSON string, status: int, progress_text}. STATUS VALUES — store strings: queued | running | succeeded | failed (jobs/store.py:15); integer mapping in responses: queued->0, running->0, succeeded->1, failed->2, anything else->2 (server_utils.py:10,118-121); a cached running record older than TASK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=3600 reports status 2 (query_result_service.py:53-54, api_server.py:111). PROGRESS: numeric, field name `progress`, float scale 0.0-1.0, inside result[0] of the parsed `result` JSON for non-terminal jobs, plus `stage` (free-text, e.g. \"queued\",\"running\", or progress descriptions) and top-level `progress_text` (last captured log line, log_capture installed at api_server.py:183). ERROR SHAPE: failed jobs => top-level status 2 and result[0].error = full Python traceback string (query_result_service.py:119-131); HTTP-level errors use FastAPI {detail: str} (400/401/415/429/503/504) while business errors inside 200 responses use the envelope {data:null, code:400|500, error: str}. RETENTION: completed jobs purged from the store after 24h (JOB_STORE_MAX_AGE_SECONDS=86400, cleanup every 300s); cache records expire after 7 days (RESULT_EXPIRE_SECONDS, api_server.py:110-113). BATCH: batch_size on /release_task defaults to 2 takes when omitted (job_generation_setup.py:203; GenerationConfig.batch_size default 2 at inference.py:243); each take is a separate element in audio_paths and a separate item in the query_result array; seed accepts comma-separated per-take values; seed_value in results is comma-joined actual seeds. OpenRouter /v1/chat/completions rides the SAME queue but waits in-request via done_event (600s timeout) or streams via progress_queue (jobs/worker_loops.py:38-45).
mapped.file_outputs: Generated audio is written to app.state.temp_audio_dir = <tmp_root>/api_audio where tmp_root = $ACESTEP_TMPDIR or <repo>/.cache/acestep/tmp — i.e. default /Users/zathras/WAVESMITH_Project/engine/ACE-Step-1.5/.cache/acestep/tmp/api_audio (lifespan_runtime.py:60-61,135-136; passed as save_dir at job_generation_runtime.py:92). Filename = deterministic UUID hash of generation params: <uuid>.<ext> (inference.py:1017-1033). Format from request `audio_format` (API default \"mp3\"; engine-level default flac): flac|mp3|opus|aac|wav|wav32 (wav32 saved with .wav extension); unsupported values fall back to flac (audio_utils.py:122-132, release_task_models.py:101-104). MP3 specifics: bitrate 128k (allowed 128k/192k/256k/320k), 48kHz (allowed 44.1k/48k) (audio_utils.py:117-120). CLIENT RETRIEVAL: the API converts each absolute path to a relative URL \"/v1/audio?path=<urllib.parse.quote(path, safe='')>\" (api_server.py:200-207); these URLs appear as `file` in /query_result items and in audio_paths/first_audio_path/second_audio_path of the success result; fetch bytes with GET /v1/audio?path=... (+ Authorization header if API key set). raw_audio_paths carries the un-encoded absolute disk paths if the client is on the same host. /v1/audio refuses (403) any path outside temp_audio_dir (audio_route.py:23-28). The OpenRouter endpoint instead inlines audio as a base64 data URL. Uploaded reference/source audio goes to system temp via tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=\"ref_audio_\"/\"ctx_audio_\") and is deleted after the job (release_task_audio_paths.py:49-93, job_runtime_state.py:64-78).
mapped.health: GET /health (no auth) always returns HTTP 200 with envelope data {status:\"ok\", service:\"ACE-Step API\", version:\"1.0\", models_initialized: bool, llm_initialized: bool, loaded_model: str|null, loaded_lm_model: str|null} (model_service_routes.py:146-161). There is NO separate readiness endpoint and NO download-progress in health. Semantics: by default ACESTEP_NO_INIT=true (startup_model_init.py:185) so the server starts instantly, accepts requests, and reports models_initialized:false / loaded_model:null until models load. Loading happens either (a) eagerly at startup if ACESTEP_NO_INIT=false / --no-init absent-equivalent (runs inside lifespan, so the server doesn't accept connections until done), (b) lazily on the FIRST queued job — CAVEAT: this path calls do_model_initialization() synchronously inside the worker coroutine (job_runtime_state.py:34-61), blocking the entire event loop, so /health may HANG (not error) during first-job model download/weights load; the affected job shows status \"queued\"/0 with progress 0.0 the whole time, or (c) explicitly via POST /v1/init, which runs in the thread executor and keeps /health responsive (model_service_routes.py:199-211). After success: models_initialized:true, loaded_model:\"acestep-v15-turbo\" (default ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH, lifespan_runtime.py:113), llm_initialized true only if the 5Hz LM was loaded (ACESTEP_INIT_LLM / GPU-tier default). If init failed, /health still says status:\"ok\" with models_initialized:false; the failure surfaces as job failures (RuntimeError with stored _init_error) or 503/500 from LM-dependent endpoints. Readiness recipe for a TS client: poll GET /health until models_initialized===true (with a request timeout, since the lazy path can stall the loop), or call POST /v1/init first. GET /v1/stats and GET /v1/model_inventory give queue depth and per-model is_loaded detail.
mapped.lm_plan_endpoint: YES — three separate callable LM steps, no DiT involved: (1) POST /v1/create_sample — description query -> full plan {caption, lyrics, bpm, keyscale, duration, timesignature, vocal_language} via 5Hz LM create_sample (sample_format_routes.py:162-246); (2) POST /format_input — query-rewrite/enhancement of existing caption+lyrics plus metadata fill via format_sample, returns {caption, lyrics, bpm, key_scale, time_signature, duration, vocal_language} (sample_format_routes.py:51-160); both lazy-init the LM and return 503 if LM loading is disabled. (3) POST /release_task with analysis_only:true — async job that runs only the LM CoT metadata plan (no audio; result has metas/bpm/keyscale/duration/prompt/lyrics, generation_info:\"Analysis Only Mode Complete\", dit_model:\"None (Analysis Only)\") (job_analysis_runtime.py:84-114); related flags full_analysis_only (audio understanding -> metadata+codes) and extract_codes_only (codes only) (job_analysis_runtime.py:38-82). Additionally POST /create_random_sample returns canned example plans without any LM call. Note: lyric generation is not a standalone endpoint beyond these — /v1/create_sample is the lyrics generator.
mapped.progress_reporting: Numeric progress IS reported: field `progress`, float 0.0-1.0 (clamped, store.py:148-157), found inside the first element of the JSON-encoded `result` array returned by POST /query_result for queued/running/failed jobs, and fixed at 1.0 in terminal success records; accompanied by `stage` (string: \"queued\", \"running\", \"succeeded\", \"failed\", or generation phase descriptions from the progress callback) and a top-level `progress_text` string per task (last server log line via the global log-capture buffer — note it is global, not per-job isolated). Updates are throttled to >=1% delta / stage change / 0.5s (job_blocking_generation.py:134-149). The store also tracks queued->running->succeeded/failed transitions; queue_position is only returned once at submit time. Async training-side tasks (/v1/dataset/preprocess_status, /v1/dataset/auto_label_status) report {status: running|completed|failed|idle, progress: str, current: int, total: int} instead. OpenRouter streaming emits SSE heartbeat chunks every 2s rather than numeric progress.
statuses: Job store status strings: queued, running, succeeded, failed (acestep/api/jobs/store.py:15)

Integer status in /query_result responses: 0 = queued or running, 1 = succeeded, 2 = failed (also returned for unknown status text, and for cached running records older than 3600s) (acestep/api/server_utils.py:10,118-121; query_result_service.py:53-54)
stage strings on records: queued, running, succeeded, failed, plus free-text generation phases via progress callback

Dataset/preprocess/auto-label async task statuses: running, completed, failed, plus idle in the latest-status endpoints (train_api_dataset_preprocess_routes.py:104,147,159; train_api_dataset_status_routes.py:29)
open_questions: The checkout at /Users/zathras/WAVESMITH_Project/engine/ACE-Step-1.5 is not a git repo per the environment, so the v0.1.8 tag pin could not be verified from inside the tree; verify the pinned source separately.

GET /v1/models is double-registered; the OpenRouter (unauthenticated, {object:'list'} format) handler shadows the authenticated wrapped-inventory handler because the router is included first (route_setup.py:70-84). A TS client must use GET /v1/model_inventory for the inventory payload. If upstream ever reorders registration, the shape of /v1/models flips — pin to /v1/model_inventory.
POST /v1/chat/completions and GET /v1/models perform NO API-key check even when --api-key is set — do not expose this server publicly assuming the key gates everything.
progress_text is the process-global last log line, not per-job; with concurrent jobs it can show another job's output.

No LRC/timestamped-lyrics and no quality-score fields exist anywhere in API responses. LRC utilities and scoring exist only in the Gradio UI layer (acestep/ui/gradio/events/results/lrc_utils.py; auto_score_time/auto_lrc_time only appear as timings inside the human-readable generation_info markdown string, generation_info.py:85-99). The separate Gradio app mounts a similar but distinct router (acestep/ui/gradio/api/api_routes.py) — not part of acestep-api.
Default batch_size=2 on /release_task (job_generation_setup.py:203): pass batch_size:1 explicitly if you want a single take; GPU-tier max batch limits exist in gpu_config and may further constrain.

Lazy model init (default ACESTEP_NO_INIT=true) blocks the asyncio event loop during first-job model load, so /health can hang during warmup — either call POST /v1/init at app start or set ACESTEP_NO_INIT=false and wait for the port to open.
audio_format values are not validated at request time; typos silently fall back to FLAC files (with the .flac extension) (audio_utils.py:129-132).
No request body size limit is enforced anywhere (only CORSMiddleware configured); base64 audio in /v1/chat/completions is bounded only by memory.

The in-memory job store + queue mean results are lost on restart except the 7-day local file cache (.cache/acestep/local_redis); job records purge 24h after completion — a client should download audio promptly and not rely on long-term polling.
CORS allows only localhost origins; a Next.js app served from another host must proxy API calls server-side (which is the plan for the TS client anyway).

verdicts.correction: Field names, defaults, aliases, Literal validations, and parsing flow are all accurate (verified against release_task_models.py:12-131, release_task_request_builder.py:32-104, release_task_param_parser.py:9-46, release_task_request_parser.py:88-205, job_generation_setup.py:203). Three inaccuracies: (1) 'Every field has aliases' is overstated — only ~30 canonical names appear in PARAM_ALIASES; the rest fall back to their own name via PARAM_ALIASES.get(name, [name]) (param_parser.py:135), though all builder-parsed fields CAN come from raw/param_obj/metas. (2) 'lm_top_p (>=1.0 treated as off)' is wrong: llm_generation_inputs.py:64-65 replaces >=1.0 with the DEFAULT 0.9, so sampling still uses top_p=0.9; only lm_top_k<=0 is truly off (top_k=None at :133). (3) audio-path validation is incomplete: besides absolute-paths-inside-system-temp, RELATIVE paths without '..' segments are accepted as-is and resolved against server CWD (release_task_audio_paths.py:40-46). Also minor: track_name arrives as "" not null when absent (builder line 100 uses parser.str).

Only the CACHE-derived success items carry the final caption/lyrics (local_cache_updates.py:61-62,86-87). (2) result[0].error with the full traceback is only returned from the STORE fallback (query_result_service.py:119-131); in the common path a failed job writes a terminal cache record WITHOUT any error field (local_cache_updates.py:115-124, written via job_execution_runtime.py:94-103), and the cache is consulted FIRST (query_result_service.py:170-182), so failed jobs typically return status 2 with result[0] = {file,wave,status,create_time,env,progress:0.0,stage:'failed'} and NO error string.

verdicts.claim: TASK LIFECYCLE: create/store record fields, asyncio.Queue + workers=1 pin, ACESTEP_QUEUE_WORKERS workers, lazy init on first job blocking loop, mark_running progress>=0.01, cache key ace_step_v1.5_<id> TTL 7d, ThreadPoolExecutor generation, throttled progress callback, success result dict fields, mark_failed full traceback, status int mapping, 24h store purge / 300s cleanup, batch semantics, OpenRouter same queue with done_event/progress_queue
verdicts.correction: Nearly all confirmed (store.py:15-205; release_task_route.py:66-85; server_cli.py:89-96 workers=1; api_server.py:109-113,194-195; job_runtime_state.py:11-61; job_execution_runtime.py:50-103; job_blocking_generation.py:132-159; worker_loops.py:9-57; inference.py:243; job_result_payload.py:54-137). One material error: 'ERROR SHAPE: failed jobs => result[0].error = full Python traceback string' is only the store-fallback shape. On failure update_terminal_job_cache writes a cache record WITHOUT an error field (local_cache_updates.py:115-124), and /query_result reads the cache first — so the traceback is normally NOT exposed via /query_result; it lives only in the in-memory record (store.mark_failed) and reaches OpenRouter clients via rec.error (worker_loops.py:41-42).

evidence.quote: error_traceback = traceback.format_exc() ... job_store.mark_failed(job_id, error_traceback); update_terminal_job_cache_fn(..., result=None, status="failed", ...)
record.progress = max(record.progress, 0.01) ... if record.status in ("succeeded", "failed"): ... now - finish_time > max_age
await rec.progress_queue.put({"type": "result", "result": rec.result}) ... rec.done_event.set()

Also note lifespan redirects TMPDIR/TEMP/TMP to tmp_root (lifespan_runtime.py:73-80), so uploaded ref/src temp files land under .cache/acestep/tmp by default too.

verdicts.claim: HEALTH narrative: no readiness endpoint, ACESTEP_NO_INIT default true, lazy first-job init blocks event loop so /health may hang, /v1/init runs in executor, reports models_initialized:false / loaded_model:null until models load, after success loaded_model='acestep-v15-turbo'
verdicts.correction: All correct EXCEPT 'loaded_model:null until models load': /health's loaded_model is inventory['default_model'] = get_model_name(app.state._config_path), and _config_path is set at startup to os.getenv('ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH','acestep-v15-turbo') — so loaded_model reports the CONFIGURED primary model name (e.g. 'acestep-v15-turbo') even while models_initialized is false. A readiness poll must key on models_initialized, never on loaded_model being non-null. ACESTEP_NO_INIT default True confirmed (startup_model_init.py:185); lazy init runs do_model_initialization synchronously inside the async worker (job_runtime_state.py:34-61); /v1/init uses run_in_executor (model_service_routes.py:196-211).

evidence.quote: primary_model = get_model_name(getattr(app.state, "_config_path", "")) ... "default_model": primary_model or None ... "loaded_model": inventory["default_model"]
app.state._config_path = os.getenv("ACESTEP_CONFIG_PATH", "acestep-v15-turbo")
no_init = env_bool("ACESTEP_NO_INIT", True)

verdicts.claim: LM PLAN ENDPOINTS: /v1/create_sample, /format_input, /release_task with analysis_only (plus full_analysis_only / extract_codes_only), /create_random_sample canned
verdicts.correction: Result dicts verified at job_analysis_runtime.py:38-114 (analysis_only result has generation_info 'Analysis Only Mode Complete', dit_model 'None (Analysis Only)', status_message 'Success'). Nuance: because analysis_only's status_message is 'Success' (not one of the two special strings), its /query_result item is reshaped by the standard cache writer (top-level bpm/keyscale folded into metas; generation_info/dit_model survive). Only full_analysis_only/extract_codes_only results pass through verbatim as result_data=[full dict].

missed: --api-key flag reliability depends on launch mode: app = create_app() executes at module import (acestep/api_server.py:357) and set_api_key reads ACESTEP_API_KEY at that instant (:191-192). Via the console script (pyproject.toml [project.scripts] acestep-api = "acestep.api_server:main") the module is imported BEFORE main() writes the flag into os.environ, and uvicorn.run("acestep.api_server:app", workers=1) reuses the cached module — so --api-key can be a silent no-op there. The shipped scripts bypass server_cli entirely: run_api_server.sh launches `python -m uvicorn acestep.api_server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8001` (note: binds 0.0.0.0, not the CLI default 127.0.0.1). A TS-client deployment should set ACESTEP_API_KEY in the environment/.env, never rely on the flag.

Failed-job tracebacks are effectively unreachable via /query_result in the normal path: failure writes a terminal cache record with NO error field (acestep/api/jobs/local_cache_updates.py:115-124) and the cache shadows the store (query_result_service.py:170-182). A client only sees status 2 + stage 'failed'. The traceback is exposed only through the store fallback (cache miss/expiry) or through /v1/chat/completions' 500 detail (rec.error).

Store-derived success items have a field-name bug: query_result_service.py:91 reads metas['caption'] but job_result_payload.py:100 stores the key 'prompt', so prompt is always "" and lyrics is the ORIGINAL (not final) lyrics in that path. Rely on the cache-derived shape (which carries final caption/lyrics plus generation_info/seed_value/lm_model/dit_model) — or better, treat both shapes as possible.

validate_audio_path accepts RELATIVE paths with no '..' segment as-is (release_task_audio_paths.py:43-46); they resolve against the server process CWD. Also, because lifespan redirects TMPDIR to <repo>/.cache/acestep/tmp (lifespan_runtime.py:73-80), the 'system temp dir' that absolute paths must live in is effectively the repo cache tmp dir after startup.

OpenRouter /v1/chat/completions silently drops audio_codes, cover_noise_strength and use_cot_metas: _to_generate_music_request accepts an audio_codes argument but never maps it (or the other two fields) onto GenerateMusicRequest (openrouter_adapter.py:321-413), so even the in-route cover auto-conversion (:752-776) is discarded. Code-string control requires /release_task's audio_code_string.
lm_top_p >= 1.0 on /release_task is replaced with the default 0.9, not disabled (acestep/api/llm_generation_inputs.py:64-65); only lm_top_k <= 0 truly disables top-k (top_k=None at :133).

Two separate concurrency knobs: ACESTEP_QUEUE_WORKERS (async queue consumers, default 1, api_server.py:195) AND ACESTEP_API_WORKERS (ThreadPoolExecutor max_workers for blocking generation, default 1, lifespan_runtime.py:117-118). Both default to 1, serializing generation.

Invalid values for pydantic-validated fields (chunk_mask_mode/repaint_mode/lm_backend Literals, repaint_strength bounds) on /release_task surface as HTTP 500, not 422, because GenerateMusicRequest is constructed manually inside the route handler (release_task_request_builder.py:104) rather than by FastAPI body validation.

LocalCache JSON-serializes lists/dicts on set (acestep/local_cache.py:68-70) — this is why /query_result's 'result' is a JSON-encoded string in both cache and store paths; it is a process-singleton diskcache persisted at .cache/acestep/local_redis (results survive restarts up to the 7-day TTL even though the job store does not).

queue_position returned by /release_task counts ALL pending jobs in the shared pending_ids deque, including jobs submitted through /v1/chat/completions (release_task_route.py:80-82; openrouter_adapter.py:789-803).

In multipart /release_task, only the exact file-field names ref_audio/reference_audio and ctx_audio/src_audio are recognized as uploads (release_task_request_parser.py:110-111); camelCase aliases work only for path-string fields.

PUT /v1/dataset/sample/{idx} force-overrides: when is_instrumental=true the server writes lyrics='[Instrumental]' and language='unknown' regardless of the submitted values (train_api_dataset_sample_routes.py:95-99).
Streaming /v1/chat/completions can finish with finish_reason 'error' followed by data: [DONE] (openrouter_adapter.py:532-536); clients that only check for 'stop' will misread failures.
/v1/audio serves .opus/.aac files with the default audio/mpeg media type — the extension map only covers .mp3/.wav/.flac/.ogg (audio_route.py:32-39).

GET /v1/dataset/scan's response contains only {message, num_samples, samples} (no dataset_name/labeled_count, unlike /v1/dataset/load) and it also sets app.state.dataset_json_path to <audio_dir>/<dataset_name>.json as the default auto-label save path (train_api_dataset_scan_load_routes.py:42-51).
